long_stay: Language courses (for programs exceeding 90 days or part of broader study plan)
qualifying_courses.notes: Short-stay Schengen visa eligibility for courses of 90 days or less depends on nationality. Language courses typically qualify if exceeding 90 days or part of a broader study plan.

booking_methods.description: Portuguese Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MNE) E-visa portal
Outsourced partner for visa application collection in many countries; applicants must register on the VFS website specific to their country.
Via official email or telephone for consular sections where VFS is not available.

steps.title: Secure Admission
steps.description: Obtain admission or acceptance from a recognized Portuguese educational institution.

steps.title: Fill Online Application Form
steps.description: Register and complete the online application form on the E-visa portal.

steps.title: Book Visa Appointment
steps.description: Schedule an appointment at the Portuguese embassy/consulate or VFS center.

steps.title: Submit Application and Attend Interview
steps.description: Submit the application in person with all original documents, provide biometric data (fingerprints), and attend an interview if requested.

steps.title: Pay Visa Fee
steps.description: Pay the required visa processing fee.

required_documents.name: Valid Passport
required_documents.details: Valid for at least 3 months beyond the intended stay, with at least two blank pages.

required_documents.name: Proof of Regular Status
required_documents.details: Required if applying from a country other than the country of origin.

required_documents.name: Criminal Record Certificate
required_documents.details: From the country of origin or residence (if residing for over a year). Must be apostilled or legalized and issued within 3 months of application.

required_documents.name: Permission for AIMA to Check Portuguese Criminal Records
Valid Travel Medical Insurance
required_documents.details: Minimum coverage of €30,000, including repatriation. Must cover the entire academic period and the whole Schengen area.

required_documents.name: Proof of Accommodation
required_documents.details: Lease agreement, university residence letter, or 'term of responsibility' from a host in Portugal.

required_documents.name: Letter of Acceptance/Enrollment
required_documents.details: Formal enrollment confirmation from a Portuguese educational institution.

required_documents.name: Proof of Financial Means
required_documents.details: Bank statements, scholarship grant, or 'term of responsibility' (sponsorship declaration).

exemptions: Students with a 'term of responsibility' signed by a Portuguese citizen/resident

residence_permit.authority_full_name: Agency for Integration, Migration and Asylum
residence_permit.booking_method: AIMA contact center (phone) or online channels, or at a 'Loja AIMA' office.
required_documents: Health insurance or proof of coverage by SNS
residence_permit.notes: Students must enter with a residence visa issued before travel; entering as a tourist and applying after arrival is no longer permitted (new rule 2026). AIMA queues in Lisbon/Porto can extend 2–4 months. The temporary receipt (comprovativo) is legally equivalent to the residence card during processing.

work_while_studying.legal_basis: Article 97 of the Foreign Nationals Law (REPSAE)
restrictions: Work must not interfere with the student's study schedule or academic performance.
Secondary school students are generally not authorized to work.

permit_renewal.online_portal: AIMA online portal (Automatic Renewal) or Portal das Renovações
required_documents: Proof of continued enrollment for new academic year
Academic transcript (proof of successful completion of previous year's subjects)

requirements: Proof of completed studies (academic degree or research project) obtained in Portugal
Valid job contract, job offer, service agreement, or proof of registering as self-employed
post_study_work.notes: After 2 years of residency, students can apply for Estatuto de Igualdade de Direitos e Deveres (equal rights to Portuguese citizens in employment and healthcare). After 5 years, can apply for permanent residence or citizenship.

official_sources.name: Ministry of Foreign Affairs – Visa Portal
official_sources.description: Visa types, fees, processing times, required documentation, legislation.

official_sources.name: AIMA (Agency for Integration, Migration and Asylum)
official_sources.description: Residence permits, renewals, work authorization, legal texts.

official_sources.name: Study in Portugal (Official Higher Education Portal)
European Commission – EU Immigration Portal, Portugal section
official_sources.description: EU-level overview, conditions to study in Portugal as a non-EU citizen.

tips.title: Apostille Documents
tips.body: Ensure criminal record and academic certificates are apostilled (Hague Convention) or legalized by the Portuguese consulate in your home country before leaving.

tips.title: Obtain NIF and Bank Account
tips.body: Apply for a Portuguese Tax Number (NIF) and open a Portuguese bank account as soon as possible, as these are often required for the residence permit.

tips.title: Health Insurance Validity
tips.body: Ensure your insurance is valid for the entire academic period, covers the whole Schengen area, and meets minimum coverage requirements.

tips.title: Application Timing
tips.body: Start the visa application at least 3-4 months before the course start date, as consulate slots fill up quickly and processing can take time.

tips.title: Consulate-Specific Requirements
tips.body: Always check the specific requirements of the consulate where you are applying, as small variations in document requirements or formats can occur between different regions.

tips.title: Check for Exemptions
tips.body: Investigate whether your specific nationality or course type qualifies for fee exemptions or simplified procedures (e.g., government scholarship holders, CPLP nationals).

common_mistakes.consequence: Can result in a rejection notice and a longer overall procedure.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Carefully review all requirements and ensure all documents are correctly filled and complete before submission.

common_mistakes.mistake: Applying too close to the intended travel or course start date
common_mistakes.consequence: Risk missing the course start date due to official decision periods (up to 90 days) plus appointment wait times.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Apply well in advance, ideally 3-4 months before your intended travel date.

common_mistakes.mistake: Underestimating the proof of means of subsistence
common_mistakes.consequence: Bank statements may not clearly show the required reference amount per month, leading to application issues.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Ensure your financial proof clearly demonstrates the required amount, which is 3x the national minimum wage per month (effective September 2026).

common_mistakes.mistake: Not booking the AIMA residence permit appointment soon enough after arrival
common_mistakes.consequence: The residence visa's entry window is limited (120 days), requiring prompt application for the residence permit.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Book your AIMA appointment immediately upon arrival in Portugal.

common_mistakes.mistake: Starting paid work without first notifying AIMA
common_mistakes.consequence: Can create irregularities affecting future residence permit renewals.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Always notify AIMA and provide necessary documentation (employment contract, Social Security registration) before commencing any professional activity.
